files.description: FRR core coding standards, PR requirements, commit message format, review process, release cycle, defensive coding, formatting rules, and architectural guidelines.
CLI/DEFPY command definition standards, VTY node architecture, help string conventions, and YANG CLI integration requirements.
MTYPE memory tracking system — XMALLOC/XCALLOC/XFREE usage, DECLARE_MTYPE/DEFINE_MTYPE patterns.
Typesafe container API (DECLARE_LIST, DECLARE_HASH, etc.) — the required replacement for legacy BSD queue macros and lib/linklist.h.
zlog API usage, printfrr format extensions (%pFX, %pI4, %pSU, etc.), and debug flag conventions.
FRR daemon architecture — event loop (threadmaster), threading model, and inter-daemon communication patterns.
Locking primitives — frr_with_mutex, frr_mutex_lock_autounlock, and concurrency guidelines.
checkpatch.sh style checker — ERROR vs WARNING classification and known exceptions.
VTYSH architecture — how CLI commands are extracted, distributed, and executed across daemons.
Top-level contribution guidelines and PR process overview.
